# /impact

> Surface existing F&O objects (X++ classes, table/form extensions, data entities, security duties, batch jobs, ER configurations) the proposed feature will touch.

## Usage

```
/impact [--feature <slug>] [--project <name>]
```

## Inputs

- `projects/{p}/d365-fo/features/{f}/spec.md`
- `agents/brownfield/_brownfield/inventory.json` (when brownfield mode)
- Sibling features under `projects/{p}/d365-fo/features/*/`

## Execution flow

1. Read spec; extract referenced object-types per [constitution/03-object-type-standards.md § 10 categories](../../constitution/03-object-type-standards.md).
2. Cross-reference brownfield inventory (when applicable) and sibling features.
3. Emit `impact.md` with:
   - **Object-types touched** per category (Data Entities / Security / Power Platform / Retail / Workflows / Business Docs / Reports / Integrations / Extensions) with risk LOW/MEDIUM/HIGH
   - **CoC extensions** — any Chain-of-Command extensions that would stack with this feature's intended changes
   - **Sibling feature conflicts** — same table-extension target, same form-extension, same security role
   - **Deployable package conflicts** — package ordering / Key Vault entries
   - **Regression surface** — DMF projects, ER configs, batch jobs to re-validate
4. No gate transitions.

## Output

- `projects/{p}/d365-fo/features/{f}/impact.md`
